report_metadata.report_title: Joint Cybersecurity Advisory: PRC State-Sponsored Cyber Actor Living off the Land to Evade Detection

file_hashes.evidence_snippet: SHA-256 file hashes (overlaps Volt Typhoon FRP)

tools.name: Earthworm
tools.evidence_snippet: Used for C2; actor proxies traffic through compromised SOHO devices

tools.name: Fast Reverse Proxy (FRP)
tools.evidence_snippet: Custom FRP client with hardcoded C2 callbacks to ports 8080/8443/8043/8000/10443; filenames: ciscoup.exe, cl64.exe, vm3dservice.exe, watchdogd.exe, Win.exe, WmiPreSV.exe, WmiPrvSE.exe

tools.name: Impacket (wmiexec.py)
tools.evidence_snippet: Redirects output to ADMIN$ share files with epoch timestamps (e.g., 1684956600.123456)

tools.name: Awen webshell
tools.evidence_snippet: Derived webshells used for persistence and exfiltration

tools.name: Secretsdump.py
tools.evidence_snippet: Component of Impacket for NTDS extraction

yara_rules.name: ShellJSP
yara_rules.description: Detects JSP webshells used by actor
yara_rules.evidence_snippet: strings: decryptfpath, decryptfcontext, decryptcommandEnc, 'upload failed!', aes.encryptallStr, newid

yara_rules.name: EncryptJSP
yara_rules.description: Detects JSP webshells used by actor
yara_rules.evidence_snippet: strings: AEScrypt, AESCBCPKCS5Padding, SecretKeySpec, FileOutputStream, getParameter, new ProcessBuilder

yara_rules.name: CustomFRPClient
yara_rules.description: Identifies actor's custom FRP tool based on unique strings

yara_rules.name: HACKTOOLFRPClient
yara_rules.description: Detects FRP tool variants (used by multiple actors)

ttps.evidence_snippet: Exploit Public-facing Application T1190 Actor used public-facing applications to gain initial access to systems; in this case, Earthworm and PortProxy.

ttps.technique: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Windows Command Shell
ttps.evidence_snippet: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Windows Command Shell T1059.003 The actor used this primary command prompt to execute a query

Server Software Component: Web Shell T1505.003 The actor used backdoor web servers with web shells to establish persistence to systems, including some of the webshells being derived from Awen webshell.
Indicator Removal T1070 The actor selectively cleared Windows Event Logs, system logs, and other technical artifacts

ttps.technique: Indicator Removal: Clear Windows Event Logs
ttps.evidence_snippet: Indicator Removal: Clear Windows Event Logs T1070.001 The actor cleared system event logs to hide activity of an intrusion.
OS Credential Dumping T1003 The actor used additional commands to obtain credentials in the environment.
Credentials from Password Stores T1555 The actor searched for common password storage locations
System Owner/User Discovery T1033 The actor gathered information about successful logons to the host using a PowerShell command.
OS Credential Dumping: NTDS T1003.003 The actor may try to exfiltrate the ntds.dit file and the SYSTEM registry hive
Windows Management Instrumentation T1047 The actor executed WMIC commands to create a copy of the SYSTEM registry.
Proxy T1090 The actor used commands to enable port forwarding on the host.
System Network Configuration Discovery T1016 The actor used commands
System Information Discovery T1082 The actor executed commands to gather information about local drives.
Permission Groups Discovery: Doman Groups T1069.002 The actor used commands to enu
Permission Groups Discovery: Local Groups T1069.001 The actor attempt to find local sy
Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell T1059.001 The actor used a PowerShell command to identify successful logons to the host.

summary: Joint CSA (NSA/CISA/FBI/ACSC/CCCS/NCSC-NZ/NCSC-UK, June 2023 v1.1) on PRC state-sponsored actor (Volt Typhoon) using LOTL post-compromise: WMIC/ntdsutil for NTDS dumping (ntds.dit + SYSTEM/SECURITY hives via Shadow Copy); netsh portproxy; PowerShell EventLog queries; Impacket wmiexec.py (output to ADMIN$[epoch timestamp]); Earthworm/FRP C2 via SOHO proxies (ASUS/Cisco/Draytek/FatPipe/Fortinet/Netgear/Zyxel). Targets critical infrastructure worldwide. IOCs: 11 SHA-256 hashes, 40+ command patterns (ntdsutil variants, 7z/rar archiving, dir \[IP].[timestamp]), 20+ filenames (ciscoup.exe, cl64.exe, etc), YARA (ShellJSP/EncryptJSP/CustomFRPClient), CVEs (2021-40539 ManageEngine, 2021-27860 FatPipe). Logging: Process creation w/cmdline (4688), WMI/PowerShell tracing, ESENT (216/325/326/327 for ntds.dit). MITIGATIONS: Harden DCs, limit portproxy, SIEM forwarding, audit admin actions.

timeline.event: Joint CSA published (PP-23-1143 Ver. 1.1); PRC actor cluster identified
